title.rendered: The Ultimate Singapore Family Itinerary (2025 Edition)

content.rendered:
A Complete Day-wise Guide for Indian Families with Kids & Vegetarians

Planning a family getaway to Singapore? You’ve come to the right place! This article will help you plan a family trip to Singapore – with all the tips from my own personal experience. Itinerary is designed for all curious travelers traveling with kids, who want a fun, practical and memorable Singapore experience — balancing adventure, sight-seeing, and those all-important pool and shopping breaks.

Before You Book Your Tickets: The Real Talk About Singapore

Yes, you’ll walk a lot. Even if you take cabs to reach your destination (which will be very costly), each attraction will require minimum half a day of walk. If you are someone not used to it, skip Singapore. But if you think you can manage (use strollers/pram for kids), then Singapore is a must-go especially for families with kids – it’s safe, clean, easily navigable, and packed with attractions that delight both adults and kids of all ages!

Day-wise Family Singapore Itinerary

Day 1: Arrival, Settle-in, and First Experiences

Your first day’s schedule depends on your arrival time in Singapore:

Morning Arrival?

  • Check into your hotel (request early check-in if possible).
  • If early check-in isn’t available, drop luggage and head straight to your hotel pool—kids will love the immediate splash!
  • Freshen up, grab an Indian meal at a nearby eatery.
  • By evening, visit the iconic Merlion and stroll Marina Bay Promenade for stunning sunset photos and the magical light show.
  • Head back early for good rest.

Evening Arrival?

  • Head to your hotel, settle in, and if energy permits, take a relaxed walk around Clarke Quay. Grab dinner at a riverside café, soak in the lights, and adjust to the local vibe.

Family Tip:

  • Always carry a change of clothes in your hand luggage for quick pool fun or freshening up if your room isn’t ready
  • Avoid Jewel Changi attractions on landing—you’ll have huge suitcases and tired kids.
  • Get good sleep tonight; tomorrow is action-packed!

Best Hotels for your trip: Check out my hotel recommendations for Singapore where I have listed 3* and 4* options with pros & cons for each

Day 2: Universal Studios Singapore – Full Thrill Day on Sentosa

Reserve a full day at Universal Studios Singapore (on Sentosa Island). Kids and grown-ups will love the amazing rides, character parades, and live shows across seven magical zones.

  • Reach Universal Studios by 10:00–10:30 AM.
  • You don’t need an expensive Express Pass if you dedicate the entire day—standard entry gets you on all major rides by closing time (usually 7:00 PM).
  • Plan the major attractions using app and waiting time: Transformation, Mummy’s revenge, Jurassic Park and Shrek 4D.
  • Use Single person lane if regular queue is too long
  • Return via Sentosa Express or RWS8 bus to Vivocity.
  • Enjoy dinner at Harborfront with views—everyone deserves a celebratory meal after this adventure!

Absolute Must-Know Tips:


🚨 Go on Weekdays Only! Avoid Saturdays, Sundays, and Singapore public holidays—queues can exceed 2+ hours.
🚨 Don’t Squeeze Other Activities Today. Universal Studios needs your full energy.
🚨 Bring refill bottles with you. There are enough points to fill drinking water
🚨 Pack Your Own Lunch! You will not find Indian food inside Universal. So carry food which you can eat while waiting in queue for the rides – sandwiches, parathas, theplas, or idlis. Also carry fruits and dry snacks as quick energy boosters. This saves time and money while maximizing ride time.

Day 3: Gardens by the Bay – The Green Heart of Singapore

After all the walking of Universal, give yourself some time to relax in the morning. Have leisurely breakfast, enjoy hotel amenities and then around 11am, pack extra pair of clothes and towels for kids and make your way to the city’s green heart: Gardens by the Bay.

What to Do:

  1. Cloud Forest (3–4 hours)
    • Walk among giant misty trees and the world’s tallest indoor waterfall.
    • Kids absolutely love splashing in the water fall, watching the dinasaurs and amazing effects available here
    • It’s air-conditioned and magical!
  2. Lunch at Gardens
    • Bismillah at Jurassic Nest Food Hall near Skytree: Amazing Indian vegetarian food with garden views.
    • Or pack a picnic and eat on the grass.
  3. Flower Dome
    • Included with your Gardens ticket.
    • Wander through exotic plants and flowers from around the world.
  4. Far East Organization Children’s Garden (FREE!)
    • Your kids will go absolutely wild here.
    • Let your kids splash and have fun in water play areas, playgrounds, and interactive zones.
    • Only kids upto 12 years are allowed in this, if kids are small, one parent is allowed with them
  5. Active Garden and PropNex Family Zone (FREE!)
    • Free playground and slides / obstacles for kids
    • Musical playground to enjoy the fun with music
  6. OCBC Skyway (Paid)
    • Walk suspended above the Supertrees.
    • Jaw-dropping skyline views.
    • Perfect for family photos.
  7. Evening Magic
    • Find a cozy spot and watch the free Supertree Light Show (there are 2 shows every night)
    • Sit on the grass with your family, soak in the ambiance.

After Gardens:

  • Walk to Marina Bay Sands area.
  • Explore The Shoppes (shopping), see ArtScience Museum, or book the SkyPark Observation Deck for panoramic Insta-worthy photos.
  • If you are interested, you can view the skyline from Singapore Flyer nearby
  • If you missed the Merlion on Day 1, it’s a short walk away.
  • Dinner options: Bayfront restaurants, lakeside cafés, or Marina Bay food courts with vegetarian selections.

Family Tip:

  • You can combine Flyer, ArtScience Museum and Cloud Forest in one day, but only if your family is energetic.
  • With so much to do, it may get overwhelming. Don’t worry. Cover what you can in a relaxed manner while enjoying what you see. We skipped Floral Fantasy, Skytree OCBC bridge and Singapore Flyer

Day 4: Sentosa Part 2 – Aquariums, Magic & Beach Fun

Post breakfast, head back to Sentosa again for full day of fun. There is so much to cover!

10:30 AM – Singapore Oceanarium

  • One of the world’s largest aquariums with over 40,000 marine animals.
  • Budget 2–3 hours of walking and exploration.
  • Kids are mesmerized by the massive tank and underwater tunnel.

Note: Skip Oceanarium if you’ve visited one in Dubai, or Thailand, or if your family isn’t enthusiastic about marine life. Alternatively, you can visit Madam Tussad’s if interested

Afternoon – Harry Potter: Visions of Magic

  • MUST-DO, even for non-Harry Potter fans!
  • Interactive wand experience where kids cast spells with their wands(literally!).
  • Walk through iconic scenes—Diagon Alley, Prophecy Room, Great Hall.
  • Time flies here—reserve 1.5–2 hours minimum.
  • Pro Tip: Book tickets online in advance; it sells out quickly!

Lunch: Visit Kailash Parbat (near Imbiah) for excellent Indian vegetarian food. Or else, you can opt for the packed food similar to Day 2.

Post-Lunch Adventures:
Pick ONE based on your family’s energy to spend the afternoon and evening:

Option A: Beach & Thrill Rides

  • Explore Imbiah Beach – soft sand, shallow water, lifeguards.
  • Try Skyline Luge Sentosa – like a go-kart meets toboggan. Kids LOVE it! (Suitable from age 4+)
  • Try iFly (indoor skydiving) – adrenaline rush for older kids.
  • Try Giant Swing – thrilling views and views!

Option B: Nature & Cable Car

  • Imbiah Beach Nature Trail – short, easy hike through tropical greenery.
  • Spot butterflies, listen to bird calls, relax away from crowds.
  • Sentosa Cable Car – glide above the island and harbor with epic city views.
  • Kids are thrilled; parents get amazing photos.

Option C: Water Play

  • Adventure Cove Waterpark – slides, lazy river, wave pool.
  • Perfect if it’s a hot day and kids need cooling off.

Once you are done with above activities, you can opt for Early Dinner. Head out to Beach Station to find Indian and Mexican vegetarian food options in Beach Station Central Bazaar.

Post dinner, dont miss the spectacular Wings of Time show

  • World-class outdoor night show with lasers, water fountains, and fire effects.
  • 15 minutes of pure magic. Arrive 30 minutes early for good seating.
  • A perfect note to end your Sentosa adventure!

Family Tip:

  • You don’t need premium tickets for Wings of Fire. Buy standard tickets and even they will give good view
  • Wear comfortable shoes; you’ll cover 4–5 km today.
  • Bring extra sunscreen, wear sunglasses and hats.

Tip: Use my code VEGSTROLLER on Klook to book Singapore activites at discounted rate.

Day 5: Animal Magic – Zoo or Night Safari

Day 5 will be scheduled basis your preference – do you want to see animals in daytime or during night.

Option 1: Singapore Zoo (Daytime)

My personal recommendation is to visit the Singapore zoo in day-time. Reach by 10.30am as the zoo closes at 6pm and you will easily need full day to cover all the sections.

  • Watch all the types of animals you know and dont know about in real – including Kangaroos, Giraffes and Zebras
  • Check the royal lions and tigers enjoying their swim or hunting the food thrown at them during feeding hours.
  • Do not miss the Seal show in the evening!!
  • Use complimentary Tram service to move from one zone to other if you are too tired.

Also, don’t miss the Kids area within Singapore zoo. You have slides, activities, games and even a complimentary water park for kids to enjoy.

You can enjoy tasty Indian meal at Copper Chimney restaurant.

Once zoo is completed, decide whether you want to visit Bird park or River wonders (River Wonders for panda-spotting and water rides) or Rainforest Kids world. We skipped these and headed back to Little India and Haji Lane for dinner and evening exploration.

Option 2: Night Safari (Evening)

  • Visit Jewel Changi airport in daytime. It is home to the world’s tallest indoor waterfall and Canopy Park — a true highlight for families
  • Spend 3-4 hours in Changi Jewel and then go to Singapore zoo for world’s first nocturnal zoo experience.
  • Guided tram rides through 7 zones with nocturnal animals.
  • Kids see creatures they’ve never seen, active at night.
  • Perfect if you have an early-morning flight the next day.
  • Budget: 2–3 hours for the tram ride.

Family Tip:

  • Zoo and Night Safari are quite far from the city center. Plan transport accordingly.
  • MRT for zoo is at Khatib which far from actual zoo. There are shuttles between Khatib and zoo. Easier and faster option is to take a cab via Grab from your hotel to zoo.

Day 6: Shopping, Culture & City Neighborhoods

This is you “Everything Else” Day – Shopping, Souvenirs & Exploration

Morning Activity (Choose One):

Option A: Science Centre Singapore

  • Hands-on exhibits kids can touch and play with.
  • Planetarium shows (check times).
  • Omni-theater with 4D films.
  • Perfect for inquisitive kids!

Option B: ArtScience Museum

  • If you didn’t visit on Day 3.
  • Unique, interactive exhibits.
  • Smaller than Science Centre; takes 1–2 hours.

Option C: Ice Cream Museum

  • Unlimited ice cream tastings!
  • Instagram-worthy backdrops.
  • Fun and quirky.

Afternoon – Shopping Circuit

Stop 1: Bugis

  • Street-level shopping for fashion, souvenirs.
  • Great for affordable shopping & local vibe.

Stop 2: Orchard Road

  • Singapore’s most famous shopping street for high end and branded purchase.
  • Malls: Orchard Central, Wisma Atria, Paragon, Ion Orchard.
  • Everything from high-end brands to local shops can be found here.

Stop 3: Little India

  • Vibrant, colorful, authentically Indian.
  • Souvenir shopping, spice markets, temple visits.
  • Restaurants for authentic Indian vegetarian dinner.

Bonus: Chinatown & Clarke Quay (if energy permits)

  • Chinatown: Heritage streets, temples, tea shops, traditional medicine shops.
  • Clarke Quay: Riverside dining, live music venues, boat rides, nightlife.
  • 45-Minute Boat Ride: Covers Singapore River, Merlion, and Marina Bay. Great way to see the city from water!

Vegetarian Dinner Ideas:

  • Little India: Saravana Bhavan, Komala Vilas
  • Chinatown: Vegetarian restaurants nearby
  • Clarke Quay: Multiple international options with veg choices
  • Orchard: Food courts in malls

Day 7: Jewel Changi – Grand Finale Before Departure

If Your Flight is Afternoon/Evening (after 4:00 PM):

  • Check out post breakfast and head to Changi Airport.
  • Check in your luggage 6-7 hours before your flight.
  • Use the free train service to Jewel terminal.
  • Store your hand baggage in lockers (sized for cabin bags, not suitcases—so check-in your main luggage first!).

If Your Flight is Early Morning:

  • Slot Jewel into an earlier day of your itinerary (maybe Day 6 morning).

What to Explore at Jewel:

  1. Rain Vortex – World’s tallest indoor waterfall (40m cascade). Absolutely stunning! Free to see.
  2. Mastercard Canopy Bridge – Will give you best view and experience of the rain vortex
  3. Hedge Maze, Mirror Maze, Bouncing Net and Walking net are other activities to do at Jewel
  4. Canopy Park – This becomes a free entry if you buy ticket for canopy bridge, hedge maze, mirror maze, bouncing net or walking net. Kids can enjoy activities like:
    • Discovery Slides
    • Foggy Bowl
    • Petal Garden
    • Topiary Walk
  5. Shopping – Last-minute souvenirs, chocolates, duty-free items
  6. Dining – For vegetarian food, go to Bikanerwala (Indian vegetarian at Terminal 3, accessible via free train)

Reserve 3-4 hours to enjoy all these highlights comfortably.

Pro Tips for Traveling with Kids to Singapore

Transport Tips

  • Get an EZ-Link Card: Reloadable card for MRT, buses, taxis. Available at airport or 7-Eleven.
  • MRT is Your Best Friend: Fast, air-conditioned, stroller-friendly, and affordable.
  • Grab or Taxis: Use Grab app for ride; sometimes cheaper than taxis.
  • Avoid Peak Hours: 7–9 AM and 5–7 PM are crowded on MRT.

Hotel Selection for Families

  • Recommended Areas: Orchard Road (shopping), Little India (culture & food), Sentosa (beaches)
  • Best 3-Star: Hotel Boss, V Hotel Lavender
  • Best 4-Star: Holiday Inn Little India, M Hotel, Royal Plaza on Scotts, Furama RiverFront
  • Best 5-Star: Marina Bay Sands, Shangri-La (kids’ club), One Farrer

Packing Essentials

  • Comfortable walking shoes (you’ll clock 10,000+ steps daily!)
  • Swimwear and beach clothes
  • Sunscreen (SPF 50+)
  • Lightweight rain jacket or umbrella
  • Sunglasses, Hat or cap for sun protection
  • Medications and first-aid kit
  • Portable charger for phones/cameras
  • Plug adapter

Money-Saving Hacks

  • Book attractions online in advance for discounts.
  • Use Klook or GetYourGuide apps for bundled deals. Use my promocode VEGSTROLLER for discounts
  • Eat at hawker centers instead of restaurants (authentic, cheap, delicious).
  • Many attractions are FREE: Gardens by the Bay (outdoor areas), Waterparks, Nature trails, Parks, Some museums on specific days.
  • Shopping malls often have free kids’ play areas!

Bonus Tip: Legoland Malaysia

Interested in Lego? Consider a one-day Legoland Malaysia package from Singapore:

  • Just 90 minutes across the border from Singapore.
  • Perfect for Lego-loving kids.
  • Many Singapore travel agents offer morning-to-evening packages.
  • Includes transport and entrance.

Singapore Family Itinerary – At A Glance

Essential Information Before You Go

Best Time to Visit

  • Year-Round: Singapore has tropical weather always (hot & humid).
  • Peak Season: December–February (slightly cooler, more crowded).
  • Festival Season: July–September (Deepavali, National Day, Mooncake Festival).
  • Rainy: November–January (brief afternoon showers; still fine to visit).

Visa Information

  • Indian Citizens: Need an e-visa (apply 3–4 weeks in advance via Singapore High Commission or authorized travel agents).
  • Processing: 3–5 business days; costs ₹2,000–₹3,000.
  • Validity: 30–63 days for tourism purposes.
  • Must-Haves: Valid passport (6+ months), hotel booking, return flight, proof of funds.

Tip: Check the cheapest flight prices on Skyscanner

So, pack your bags, charge those cameras, and prepare to create memories that will last a lifetime.

Have you been to Singapore? Share your family tips and favorite spots in the comments below—let’s help other families plan their perfect trip!

Happy travels! 🌴✈️🎡

excerpt.rendered: Planning a family getaway to Singapore? This article will help you plan a family trip to Singapore – with all the tips from my own personal experience.

content.rendered:
Singapore is a costly country for tourists seeking good accommodation. Room sizes are very small but prices are very high. This made me do extensive research on all the good hotels in Singapore as part of my next travel.

Basis this, I am sharing stay recommendations for Singapore. You will find Instragram-worthy hotels, budget hotels, luxury hotels, hotels near shopping areas and much more.

Do remember to share your thoughts and which of these you like the most

Singapore - 3* Properties

Name -
Hotel Boss

Location - Lavender/Bugis

Type – Hotel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • Favorite of Indian Tour Agents
  • Budget Friendly
  • Large property with swimming pool
  • Will get Indian food options in buffet breakfast
  • 7 minutes walk to nearest MRT (Lavender MRT)
  • Near to Little India
  • Near to Indian restaurants

Things To Know

  • Small rooms
  • Can get crowded
  • Basic amenities
  • Limited breakfast for vegetarian

Name - V Lavender

Location - Lavender

Type – Hotel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • Budget Friendly
  • Large property with swimming pool
  • Adjacent to nearest MRT (Lavender MRT)
  • Modern hotel with multiple dining options nearby

Things To Know

  • Being adjacent to MRT, the surroundings are usually busy

Name - Ibis Budget Singapore Gold

Location - Geylang

Type – Hotel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • Very budget friendly
  • Clean property with simple rooms
  • 15 minutes walk to nearest MRT (Kellong)
  • Indian food options available nearby

Things To Know

  • Rooms and Bathrooms are small
  • Limited amenities
  • Located in Geylong (night life and red light area may not suit families)

Singapore - 4* Properties

Name -
Aloft Novena

Location - Novena

Type – Hotel

Hotel & Room Tour
Book This Property

What We Liked

  • We stayed here in August 2025 🙂
  • Marriott property
  • Family friendly and large hotel with 2 wings
  • Offers free breakfast for kids below 12 years
  • Has 2-3 Indian options in breakfast
  • This hotel has 2 different bus stops and both are 2 – 3 min walk
  • Central location allows easy access to all major attractions
  • 7-11, Macdonalds, Starbucks and Zongshun mall in vicinity
  • Complimentary shuttle service between hotel and Novena MRT station
  • Good swimming pool, gym
  • Has laundry room with pay per use option
  • Has a small billiards table in lobby
  • Has few slides for children to play
  • Vast grass area to walk/play/sit
 
 

Things To Know

  • Same Indian breakfast is served everyday and you will get bored
  • Veg options in breakfast are limited only to Idli, Dosa, Maida paratha, kidney beans (rajma), breads, fruits, juice and milk
  • 15 min walk to Novena MRT station

Name -
Royal Plaza on Scots

Location - Orchard Road

Type – Hotel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • Our friend stayed here in July 2025
  • Spacious and modern rooms
  • Large options of breakfast including Indian food
  • 2 – 3 minutes walk to Orchard MRT station
  • Children upto 11 years can stay free with parents on existing bedding

Things To Know

  • Premium rates due to location

Name -
Holiday Inn

Location - Little India

Type – Hotel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • Variety of Indian food options in breakfast
  • Family friendly hotel
  • Has Swimming Pool and Gym
  • Walkable to Little India MRT station
  • 2 minutes walk to nearest MRT (Ferrer Park)
  • Near to Little India
  • Near to Indian restaurants

Things To Know

  • Can become costly on long weekends / peak tourist seasons
  • Can get crowded

Name -
Hilton Garden Inn

Location - Serangoon

Type – Hotel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • Modern Clean rooms
  • Very near to Mustafa Center and Little India MRT
  • Variety of Indian food options in breakfast
  • Family friendly hotel
  • Has Swimming Pool and Gym
  • Walkable to Little India MRT station
  • Near to Indian restaurants

Things To Know

  • Small swimming pool
  • Is mostly sold out

Name -
Serangoon House Little India

Location - Little India

Type – Hotel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • Marriott property
  • 4 – 5 minutes from Ferrer MRT station
  • Indian style decor
  • Variety of Indian food options
  • Offers child friendly breakfast
  • Has bunker style bed for 3 or 4 member family

Things To Know

  • Small rooms
  • No Swimming pool or amenity
  • Premium cost for the room size

Name -
Yotel

Location - Orchard Road

Type – Hotel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • We had booked this hotel initially but later changed due to room size
  • Award winning hotel
  • Compact but beautiful rooms with every inch of space being utilized
  • High focus on cleanliness
  • Perfect for couple or family with 1 kid
  • Bed which can roll up to become sofa at click of a button
  • Great views from bathroom 🙂
  • Decent buffet with few Indian options
  • Few minutes walk to MRT station
  • Perfect hotel if you love brands and shopping – this is in the heart of shopping district – near to all the premier shopping places, upscale malls and international brands 

Things To Know

  • Rooms are very tiny
  • Don’t book if you are traveling with multiple luggage
  • Not suitable for family of 4 or more
  • Small swimming pool
  • 3 people family should choose loft room as it will give extra bed for 3rd person
  • Limited room service
  • Affordable for the location

Name -
M Social

Location - Robertson Quay

Type – Hotel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • Instagram worthy hotel
  • Award winning hotel
  • Very beatiful lobby and hotel sitauted near riverside
  • Great for couples
  • Great for anyone interested in fun and social places
  • Modern Clean rooms

Things To Know

  • Small swimming pool
  • Found breakfast expensive
  • About 12 min walk to Havelock MRT 

Name -
M Studio

Location - Robertson Quay

Type – Hotel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • Instagram worthy hotel
  • Very beatiful lobby and hotel sitauted near riverside
  • Great for couples
  • Unique loft style rooms
  • Modern Clean rooms

Things To Know

  • Climbing steps for loft may be inconvenient for few people
  • Room size is small
  • About 14 min walk to Havelock MRT

Name -
Furama Riverfront

Location - Havelock

Type – Hotel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • Our parents stayed here in March 2025
  • Family Friendly and large hotel
  • Has multiple type of rooms to suit different group sizes
  • Bigger rooms
  • Large swimming pool
  • Has kids play area and kids buffet
  • Has Indian food options in breakfast
  • 8 min walk to Havelock MRT

Things To Know

  • Slightly older interiors

Name -
Holiday Inn Express

Location - Orchard Road

Type – Hotel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • Free breakfast
  • Family friendly hotel
  • Variety of Indian food options in breakfast
  • Family friendly hotel
  • Has Swimming Pool and Gym
  • Near to all the premium shopping centers

Things To Know

  • 10-12 min walk to Orchard MRT station

Name -
M Hotel

Location - Singapore City Center

Type – Hotel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • Big rooms
  • Very clean property
  • Attentive and friendly staff
  • 5 min walk to Tanjong Pagar MRT station
  • Kids friendly breakfast menu
  • Family friendly hotel
  • Variety of Indian food options in breakfast
  • Has Swimming Pool and Gym

Things To Know

  • Can get crowded during check-in / check-out

5* options you can consider in Singapore (did not elaborate on them as they were out of my budget :))

Village Hotel Sentosa

More Details

W Singapore

More Details

Marina Bay

More Details

Fullerton Hotel

More Details

title.rendered: 🌍 26-Day Europe Itinerary for Indian Families: From Paris to the Alps and Beyond

content.rendered:
Planning your first family trip to Europe from India? Overwhelmed by where to start, what to see, and how to manage it all with kids?

We were too — until we made it happen. Our 26-day self-planned Europe itinerary covered France, Germany, Switzerland, Austria, and a Mediterranean cruise, and was tailored for a vegetarian Indian family with kids. This guide is your detailed companion to plan a stress-free, memorable vacation.

🧳 Pre-Trip Essentials

  • Schengen Visa (via VFS Global): Apply 6–8 weeks in advance
  • Multi-city flights: We landed in Paris and flew out from Paris again
  • Train travel: Used Eurail Global Pass and Swiss Travel Pass
  • Stay options: Airbnb and Apartment Hotels for family-friendly flexibility
  • Packing: Instant Indian meals, dry snacks, layers for weather changes
  • Travel Insurance & Forex Card: Non-negotiable

📅 Day-by-Day Europe Travel Itinerary

🇫🇷 Day 1–3: Exploring Paris with Family

Day 1:

  • Arrived in Paris
  • Checked into our Airbnb
  • Bought groceries & explored the neighborhood
  • Took it slow and relaxed after the long flight

Day 2:

  • Visited Eiffel Tower during the day
  • Took a scenic stroll along the Seine River (we skipped river cruise as we had planned proper cruise)
  • Returned at night to watch Eiffel Tower twinkle – magical for kids!

Day 3:

  • Revisited Eiffel Tower for different photo ops
  • Explored Louvre Museum from outside (choose skip-the-line tickets if you plan to visit from outside!)
  • Visited Arc de Triomphe
  • Enjoyed ice skating near Champs-Élysées

💡 Tip: Paris is best explored at a relaxed pace — especially with children. Metro is super convenient and stroller-friendly.

Do check out my Paris Accommodation Recommendations for amazing stay options

🇩🇪 Day 4–6: Black Forest & Lake Titisee in Germany

Day 4:

  • Took a direct Eurail train to Freiburg im Breisgau (~4 hours)
  • Checked into a hotel and explored a nearby park
  • Note: Sundays are quiet in Germany — most shops are closed

Day 5:

  • Explored Freiburg Im Breisgau: University area, Münster Cathedral, local bakeries
  • Soaked in the peaceful vibe of this eco-friendly city
  • Note: Explore Freiburg Im Breisgau on cycles or public trams. They are cheap and fun

Day 6:

  • Rented a self-drive car and visited Lake Titisee
  • Continued to Black Forest region — fairy-tale forests and winding roads
  • Hindsight: Better to split Lake Titisee and Black Forest over two days

🚗 Tip: Driving in Germany is smooth — carry an International Driving Permit

💡 Tip: Most things in Black Forest close by 5.30-6pm. So plan your trip accordingly

Do check out my Freiburg Im Breisgau Accommodation Recommendations for amazing stay options

🇨🇭 Day 7–10: Switzerland – Interlaken, Jungfraujoch, Lucerne

Day 7:

  • Took direct Eurail Train to Interlaken (~3 hours), checked into apartment
  • Explored the picturesque village of Lauterbrunnen

Day 8:

  • Day trip to Lucerne: Lion Monument, Chapel Bridge
  • Tried Swiss chocolates & explored the old town

Day 9:

  • Visited Jungfraujoch – Top of Europe
  • Also explored Grindelwald – cable cars, toboggans, and views

Day 10:

  • Local shopping and relaxation in Interlaken

🍫 Tip: Switzerland is kid-friendly, clean, and scenic. Use the Swiss Travel Pass for maximum value.

Do check out my Interlaken Accommodation Recommendations for amazing stay options

🚢 Day 11–16: Mediterranean Cruise – MSC Experience

Day 11:

  • Train to Milan, then to Genoa
  • Overnight stay before cruise boarding

Day 12:

  • Boarded the MSC Cruise — all set for fun at sea!

Day 13:

  • Cruise ported at Marseille, France – local sightseeing

Day 14:

  • Docked at Barcelona, Spain – visited Sagrada Familia, local tapas

Day 15:

  • Full day at sea – pools, kids club, magic shows & fine dining

Day 16:

  • Disembarked at Genoa
  • Took a train to Milan for quick sightseeing
  • Caught an overnight express train to Innsbruck, Austria

🛳️ Tip: Cruises are perfect for families — no daily packing, food included, tons of activities!

Do check out my Genoa Accommodation Recommendations for amazing stay options

🇦🇹 Day 17–20: Alpine Adventures in Innsbruck, Austria

Day 17:

  • Arrived in Innsbruck, checked in to our Apartment hotel and explored the town center
  • This was our best property on entire Europe tour

Day 18:

  • Hiking trails, cable cars, and snow views
  • Kids loved the mountain play areas

Day 19:

  • Day trip to the magical Swarovski Crystal Worlds Museum

Day 20:

  • Relaxed local sightseeing and preparing for the next leg

⛰️ Tip: Innsbruck is less crowded than Swiss towns but equally stunning.

Do check out my Innsbruck Accommodation Recommendations for amazing stay options

🎢 Day 21–25: Disneyland Paris Finale

Day 21:

  • Took a train back to Switzerland, then bullet train to Paris
  • Checked into an Airbnb near Disneyland Paris

Day 22–23:

  • 2 full days in Disneyland – meet & greets, parades, fireworks
  • FastPass and stroller rental highly recommended

Day 24:

  • Free day for souvenir shopping or extra sightseeing in Paris

Day 25:

  • Flight back to India with a heart full of memories

🎠 Tip: Choose a weekday for Disneyland to avoid long queues. Bring refillable water bottles and snacks for kids.

Do check out my Paris Accommodation Recommendations for amazing stay options

✅ Why This Europe Itinerary Works for Indian Families

  • 🧒 Kid-friendly pace & activities
  • 🥗 Vegetarian food options available or manageable
  • 🚆 Easy train connections using Eurail
  • 🏡 Comfortable stays via Airbnb
  • 🇮🇳 Practical for first-time Indian travelers

🔑 Must-Know Europe Travel Tips for Indian Families

  • Download offline Google Maps
  • Carry passport photocopies & visa printouts
  • Use Google Lens/Translate for menus & signs
  • Learn a few local phrases – kids will love it!
  • Keep buffer days for flexibility

🌟 Final Thoughts

From the magic of the Eiffel Tower to the charm of Innsbruck, and from the Swiss Alps to the Mediterranean Sea — this Europe itinerary is doable, delightful, and unforgettable for Indian families.

We hope our experience helps you plan yours with confidence. Have questions? Drop them in the comments or follow our journey on Instagram and YouTube!

excerpt.rendered: Planning your first family trip to Europe from India? Overwhelmed by where to start, what to see, and how to manage it all with kids? We were too — until we made it happen. Our 26-day self-planned Europe itinerary covered France, Germany, Switzerland, Austria, and a Mediterranean cruise, and was tailored for a vegetarian Indian […]

title.rendered: The Ultimate Credit Card Hack: How I Get Free Flights and 5-Star Hotel Stays Every Year

content.rendered:
Before diving into the details, here’s what you need to know: I’ve developed a system that gets me 6-7 free domestic flights and luxury hotel stays worth ₹40,000 annually, all by strategically using just two credit cards and some smart spending habits. This strategy requires spending about ₹9 lakhs annually (which many families do anyway) and paying just ₹6,500 in annual fees.

The Pain of Expensive Travel

Hey there, money-savvy friends! Welcome back to my site. If you’re anything like me, you love traveling but hate what it does to your bank account. Right?

Let’s be honest – the two biggest expenses when planning any vacation are flights and accommodations. You find a great destination, get excited, and then boom – reality hits when you see those flight prices and hotel rates. Suddenly your dream vacation starts looking more like a financial nightmare.

But what if I told you that I haven’t paid full price for a domestic flight or luxury hotel stay in years? No, I’m not breaking any laws or doing anything shady. I’m just being smart about which credit cards I use and how I use them.

My Two-Card Strategy for Travel Hacking

I’m going to share my exact system using just two credit cards that literally funds my family vacations every year. This isn’t one of those “sign up for 20 credit cards” schemes. It’s simple, sustainable, and incredibly effective.

Card #1: SBI Club Vistara Credit Card

The first card in my arsenal is the SBI Club Vistara Credit Card. Yes, it has an annual fee of ₹1,499, but hear me out before you click away

When you spend ₹5 lakhs in a year on this card (which many families easily do on regular expenses), you can earn up to 5 free Air India domestic flight tickets! You just pay the taxes, which are around ₹700 per ticket. Compare that to the full ticket price of approximately ₹8,000, and you’re saving ₹7,300 per ticket

But that’s not all! You also get:

  • A ₹5,000 hotel stay voucher redeemable through Yatra
  • Reward points that can get you 1-2 additional flight tickets

Now, the good news is that Tata group and SBI have decided to keep the benefits same. So only my flight changed from Vistara to Air India. Rest everything is same.

Card #2: American Express Platinum Travel Card

The second card I swear by is the Amex Platinum Travel Card. It has a slightly higher annual fee of ₹5,000, but the benefits are absolutely worth it

When you spend ₹4 lakhs on this card annually, you get:

  • A Taj Experiences E-Gift Card worth ₹10,000 – hello, luxury stay!

15,000 bonus Membership Rewards points when you spend ₹1.9 lakhs

An additional 25,000 bonus points when you reach ₹4 lakhs in spending

I convert these points to hotel vouchers worth approximately ₹20,000. At 50 paise per point value, those 40,000 points are extremely valuable for luxury stays

Quick side note: Look at me pretending I’m not excited about free airport lounge access with both these cards. Fancy coffee and snacks before flights? Yes, please!

The Math Behind the Magic

Let’s break down the value proposition:

  • Annual card fees: ₹1,499 + ₹5,000 = ₹6,499
  • Required spending: ₹9 lakhs (which many families spend anyway on daily expenses)
  • Value received: 6-7 flight tickets (worth approximately ₹56,000) + hotel vouchers worth ₹35,000
  • Total value: Approximately ₹91,000 for a ₹6,499 investment

That’s a return of about 1,400% on your annual fee investment. Show me another investment with that kind of return!

Advanced Hacks to Maximize Your Rewards

Hack #1: Tata Neu Ecosystem

One clever trick I use is concentrating my everyday spending within the Tata ecosystem. I use:

  • BigBasket for groceries
  • 1mg for medicines and health products
  • Croma for electronics
  • Westside and Tata Cliq for clothing

This way, I earn Tata Neu Coins on every purchase. Remember: 1 NeuCoin = 1 Rupee in value

These points add up quickly when you’re spending ₹9 lakhs annually, and I typically earn enough for another free night at a Taj hotel.

Hack #2: The Voucher Arbitrage

Here’s a hack not many people know about. Amex isn’t accepted everywhere, right? Frustrating! But there’s a workaround:

When shopping at stores that don’t accept Amex, I quickly purchase a digital voucher for that store through the Amex Rewards Multiplier portal. Then I simply show the voucher code to the cashier. This accomplishes three things:

  1. I still get to use my Amex card indirectly
  2. I earn extra reward points through the multiplier portal
  3. I often get additional discounts on the vouchers themselves

It’s like getting paid to shop at places that don’t even accept your card!

Hack #3: Link Everything Possible

I’ve linked my Amazon account and Uber to these cards for automatic payments. For services that don’t allow direct linking, I purchase vouchers through the rewards portal. This ensures every rupee I spend is working toward my free travel goals.

Real-World Impact

Let me paint a picture of what this means in real life. Last year, my family of four flew to Goa for a long weekend. The flights would have cost us around ₹32,000, but we paid just ₹2,800 in taxes. We stayed at a Taj property for three nights using our vouchers – a stay that would have normally cost ₹45,000.

Total savings for one weekend trip: ₹74,200!

Common Questions You Might Have

“Isn’t it complicated to keep track of all this?”
Not really! I spend maybe 15 minutes a month ensuring I’m routing expenses to the right card.

“What if I don’t spend ₹9 lakhs annually?”
The strategy still works at lower spending levels – you’ll just accumulate rewards more slowly. Even spending half that amount will get you significant benefits.

“Are these cards worth the annual fee?”
If you use the benefits, absolutely! Just one redeemed flight ticket more than covers the SBI card fee, and one night at a Taj hotel easily covers the Amex fee.

Conclusion

Travel doesn’t have to destroy your budget. With strategic credit card usage and smart spending habits, you can enjoy significant savings on flights and luxury accommodations.

Remember, this isn’t about spending more money – it’s about getting more value from the money you’re already spending. By channeling your existing expenses through the right cards and using these hacks, you can turn everyday purchases into extraordinary travel experiences.

If you found this useful, hit that like button and subscribe for more money-saving strategies. Also, drop a comment below and let me know which hack you’re most excited to try!

Until next time, happy travels and even happier savings!

This strategy requires spending about ₹9 lakhs annually (which many families do anyway) […]

Hotel & Room Tour
Book This Property

Things To Know

Things To Know

Things To Know

Lonavala

Name - Goldfinch villa by Aaramghar Stays

Location - Lonavla Old Highway

Type – Bungalow / Villa

Hotel & Room Tour
Book This Property

What We Liked

  • We stayed here in 2025 🙂
  • Temperature Controlled Pool 🙂
  • Neat, clean and spacious property
  • Amazing meals
  • 4 Bedrooms which can accommodate max 12 people
  • Games – Snooker, Poker, Carroms, Uno, cards etc
  • Walkable to old Mumbai-Pune highway

Things To Know

  • Queen beds can fit only 2 adults. So ask for extra bed in each room to accommodate 3 per room
  • One bedroom is on 1st floor and three bedrooms are on 2nd floor. So people with elders/knee problem should avoid
  • Highway is nearby. Light sleepers may get disturbed

  • Can enjoy bonfire, books and board games available at property

  • Centrally located with close access to most of the things
  • This is a business hotel, so there is no activity room for children
  • Offers spa, gym and swimming pool

Things To Know

  • Do not miss their high tea if it is included in their package. Large spread and yummy dishes, enough to make you skip dinner 🙂

Name - Taj Santa Cruz

Location- Mumbai (Santacruz)

Type – 5* Hotel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • We stayed here in 2023 🙂
  • Its a Taj 🙂
  • Very close to Mumbai airport
  • Few rooms have airport runway view. So you can literally see planes landing/taking off
  • Windows were sealed and there was no noise of aeroplane inside the rooms
  • Huge rooms with luxurious amenities and fittings in room and bathroom
  • Beautiful lobby for Insta worthy photos
  • Centrally located with close access to most of the things

Things To Know

  • Few rooms do not have any view and hence be careful while booking if this is a criteria for you.

Name - The Fern Residency

Location- Mumbai (Chembur)

Type – 5* Hotel

Hotel & Room Tour
Book This Property

What We Liked

  • We stayed here in Jan 2026 🙂
  • Good hotel with complimentary access to club and lots of amenities
  • Indoor games – 3 TT tables, 2 Squash court, 2 Billiards table, Carroms, Ludo and many other board games
  • Outdoor games – Badminton, Tennis, Pickleball, Football/Cricket Enclosed Turf
  • Other amenities – Swimming pool, Gymnasium, Children play area with swings, slides and everything, Library with good collection of books, park for jogging/walking
  • Our suite room was very big for Mumbai standards and easily fitted extra bed
  • Decent food and breakfast
  • Hotel is in a quiet lane and hence free from noise and traffic
  • Centrally located hotel and within a very safe and good neighborhood

Things To Know

  • Hotel only has rooms. It has tie-up with Acres Club which is bang opposite service road. Hence, residents can enjoy all the amenities of club for free
  • They do provide racquet and shuttle/ball for TT/Badminton
  • For squash, they insist on wearing rubber sole shoes and bringing own racquet/ball.
  • There is a school in nearby building and may cause some traffic during school start/end time

Kashid

Name - Prakruti Resort

Location- Kashid

Type – Resort

Book This Property

  • Beach facing property
  • Offers Spa, Swimming Pool, parks

Book This Property

Name - Parc Estique

Location - East Pune (Viman Nagar)

Type – Hotel

Hotel & Room Tour
Book This Property

What We Liked

  • We stayed here in Feb 2025 🙂
  • Its a 4* business hotel
  • 3 min walk to Phoenix mall
  • Close proximity to all happening places of East Pune (Airport, IT Parks, Malls, Cafe, Pubs, Eating Joints, KP, Kalyani Nagar etc)
  • Big rooms and rooftop swimming pool
  • Decent breakfast

Things To Know

Name - The Green Gate Resort

Location - Mulshi

Type – Resort

Hotel & Room Tour
Book This Property

What We Liked

  • We stayed here in 2025 🙂
  • Amazing property with beautiful view of Mulshi lake from each room
  • Each room offers a balcony to sit and enjoy the views of Mulshi lake
  • Large, clean and comfortable rooms even in Deluxe category
  • Each room can accommodate 1-2 extra beds easily if required (on extra payment basis)
  • Pure veg in-house restaurant offering good taste and decent choices
  • Indoor Sport room which has Foosball, Snooker, Carrom and Table Tennis
  • Best suited to relax for a day or two
  • Private Swimming Pool for Villas and Common  Swimming Pool for other categories

Things To Know

  • No/Weak Mobile Network in the property
  • Unsecured Wifi of hotel
  • Villas are situated little uphill. Hotel provides pickup & drop facility till reception/restaurant
  • Does not have swings/play area for toddlers/small kids

Name - Serene Eco Village

Location - Dhanep

Type – Resort

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • We stayed here in 2024 🙂
  • Amazing property with focus on healthy, sustainable living and eco tourism
  • Large, clean and comfortable rooms
  • Pure veg in-house restaurant offering good taste and decent choices
  • Activities include trail walks to nearby dam, tour of their farm, trek to Torna fort, mountain and dam backwater trek, bonfire in night etc
  • They have tree houses, swings and pet zone/birds area to keep the kids occupied
  • They offer staycation package which focuses on helping cure ailments and promoting medicine free life
  • You can choose from one day picnic, overnight stay or multiple days staycation package

Things To Know

  • Weak Mobile Network in some areas

Name - Gypsy Soul

Location - Panshet

Type – Resort

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • We stayed here in 2024 🙂
  • Easy to reach from Pune
  • Good experience for one night stay even for kids and families
  • It has multiple stay options right from glam camping to regular camps and proper rooms
  • Live music, Open air movie show, star gazing, fireflies watch during season are the highlights of stay
  • Complimentary midnight Maggi 🙂
  •  Trek and Hike options nearby
  • Options to play foosball, cricket, carroms, chess, badminton
  • Ample parking space

Things To Know

  • Pick stay which offers private bathroom if you are traveling with family / kids

Name - Prana Resort

Location - Ravet

Type – Resort

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • We stayed here in Dec 2025 🙂
  • Easy to reach from Pune
  • Good experience for one night stay even for kids and families
  • It has two type of stay options – tents and rooms. Rooms are near swimming pool and tent is near adventure zone/restaurant
  • Beautiful ambience in night and Live music every night
  • Has adventure activities like burma bridge, zipline, ferris wheel, giat swig, rock climbing etc on premises
  • Resident guests can enjoy complimentary indoor games like Airhockey, foosball, billiards, chess, carroms
  • Have swings and slides for small kids
  • Open air restaurant (RiverDeck) was near lakeside and served tasty food
  • Rooms had a queen size bed and a sofa which could be converted to sofa cum bed with extra bedding (check if it is chargeable or complimentary)
  • Ample parking space

Things To Know

  • Current restaurant (Riverdeck) serves both veg and non veg food. 
  • Upcoming restaurant (Jalsa) will cater only to veg food
  • Adventure activities were charged extra even for resident guests
  • You can also visit this place for day trip (adventure activities) or only for dinner

Name - Oxford Golf Resort

Location - Lavale, Bavdhan

Type – Resort

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • We stayed here in Feb 2026 🙂
  • Easy to reach from Pune
  • Good experience for one night stay even for kids and families
  • All the rooms are suites which include sofa cum bed in living room and double bed/twin bed in bedroom.
  • Rooms have a small kitchen area with microwave, induction stove, chimney. But cutlery is not provided.
  • Rooms have a separate w/c toilet apart from regular toilet+bath.
  • USP – Amazing golf course (though access is allowed only for professional golfers). Instead you will have to settle by hitting golf balls from one area (which is still fun)
  • Indoor games – TT tables (2), badminton court (2), pickleball court, squash court, chess, carroms
  • All sports equipment are provided at the resort
  • Outdoor games – Children play area
  • Other amenities – Library with huge collection of books, Swimming pool, Gymnasium
  • Decent food, ample parking space

Things To Know

  • About 2-3 km inside from main road with nothing else nearby. So you will be restricted to eating meals within hotel. Hence, advisable to add all meals to the booking.
  • Resort has strict policy of max 3 guest per room while booking. If the child is between 5 – 12 and will use existing beds, then resort allows it on extra payment of 2500/-++ directly at the premises
  • They do not allow you to play TT/Squash/Badminton/Pickleball until you are in sports attire. 

Other nearby options you can consider in Pune

Novotel (East Pune)

Book This Property

Hyatt (East Pune)

Book This Property

Ritz Carlton (East Pune)

content.rendered:
Sharing recommendations for following cities of North India

  • Leh (Ladakh)
  • Rishikesh (Uttarakhand)

Leh

Name - Pal Hotel

Location - Leh

Type – 4* Hotel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • My wife stayed here in April 2025 during her girls trip 🙂
  • Centralized heating making it a perfect hotel for cold season
  • Beautiful, neat and clean 4* property
  • Walkable to Leh market
  • View of Himalayas from the room
  • In house restaurant and good food
  • Hot water facility in each room
  • Luggage storing facility
  • Ample parking space for both bikes and cars
  • 10 – 15 min walk to Leh market
  • Oxygen cylinders available
  • Cab available on request

Things To Know

  • With central heating and heavy blankets, first night it got a bit too warm for girls. Had to open window to allow fresh air

Name - Togochey

Location - Leh

Type – Hotel

Hotel & Room Tour
Book This Property

What We Liked

  • I stayed here in 2024 🙂
  • Neat and clean property
  • View of Himalayas from the room
  • In house restaurant and good food
  • Hot water facility in each room
  • Luggage storing facility
  • Ample parking space for both bikes and cars
  • 10 – 15 min walk to Leh market
  • Oxygen cylinders available
  • Cab available on request

Things To Know

  • Going to Leh market is easy as it is downhill. But walking back uphill to hotel may be difficult for few people
  • Opens only from May

Name - Zostel

Location - Leh

Type – Hostel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • I stayed here in 2024 🙂
  • Perfect for solo travelers
  • Neat and clean property
  • Sharing/Non sharing rooms fitting every budget
  • Board games, books to relax
  • View of Himalayas from the rooftop cafeteria
  • Can meet like minded people / easily find people to form groups & explore Ladakh
  • In house restaurant and good food
  • Hot water geysor in each room
  • 7-10 min walk to Leh market
  •  

Things To Know

  • 3 – 4 floors without lift and narrow stairs
  • Limited parking space for 2 wheelers on first come first serve basis
  • Opens only from May

Name - Sia-La Guest House

Location - Leh

Type – Hostel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • My wife stayed here in April 2024 during her girls trip 🙂
  • Perfect for one – two nights
  • Neat and clean property
  • Offer heaters in the room during cold season to help you stay warm
  • Served hot and delicious parathas in breakfast
  • Hot water geysor in each room
  • 5-10 min walk to Leh market
  •  

Things To Know

  • Hospitality – The girls reached Sia Residency which is a different hotel at 10pm in night. They called owner of Sia-La Guest house and he kindly offered to reach there in his car, pick up the girls and take them to right hotel at no extra cost
  • 1-2 floors without lift and narrow stairs
  • Limited parking space for 2 wheelers on first come first serve basis

Name - Organic Boutique Hotel

Location - Hunder, Nubra

Type – Hotel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • My wife stayed here in April 2024 during her girls trip 🙂
  • Good for one – two nights
  • Hot water available in washroom
  • Neat and clean property
  • Offer heaters in the room during very cold season to help you stay warm
  • Near to Hunder Sand Dunes 

Things To Know

  • Hospitality – The girls were promised heaters in the room while booking. But they did not give them heaters citing it is not cold. It became a uncomfortable night for the girls. So ensure you speak with owner and clear everything before checking-in
  • Prefer rooms instead of tents in cold season

Name - Thangzing House

Location - Pangong (Man)

Type – Homestay

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • My wife stayed here in April 2024 during her girls trip 🙂
  • She found it the best of her Ladakh trip stays
  • Beautiful view of Pangong lake from rooms
  • Warm hosts who take all care of your needs
  • Neat and clean property
  • Offer heaters in the room during cold season to help you stay warm
  • Served hot and delicious food

Things To Know

  • This is located in Man village which is little ahead of Shooting point. Ensure you tell your driver and agree on charges accordingly
  • Washroom has Indian toilet

Other nearby options you can consider in Leh

Grand Dragon (Leh)

Book This Property

Organic Village Retreat (Hunder)

Book This Property

Rishikesh

Name - Trihari

Location - Chauda Bigha, Rishikesh

Type – Hotel

Hotel Review
Book This Property

What We Liked

  • We stayed here in 2024 🙂
  • Ample car parking space (biggest advantage)
  • 3* property in close proximity to all places of importance
  • Shared auto available to Triveni Ghat / Laxman Jhula
  • Neat and clean rooms with a/c or non a/c options
  • In house restaurant with decent food
  • Limited choice buffet breakfast (Sandwich, Aloo Puri, Poha, Idli, Tea/Coffee)
  • Lift facility

Things To Know

  • Wifi was patchy in few rooms

excerpt.rendered:
Sharing recommendations for following cities of North India –

1) Leh (Ladakh)
2) Rishikesh (Uttarakhand)

content.rendered:
Sharing recommendations for following cities of Thailand

  • Bangkok
  • Koh-Samui
  • Pattaya
  • Phuket

Bangkok

Name - Bangkok City Suite

Location - Siam

Type – Hotel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • We stayed here in 2020 🙂
  • Small but neat and clean property
  • Near to 7-11 store, fruit market and tourist attractions
  • Walkable to metro station

Things To Know

  • Buffet Breakfast does not have too many options for Indian vegetarians

Name - A-One Bangkok Hotel

Location - Huey Kwang

Type – Hotel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • Neat and clean 4* hotel
  • Swimming Pool, Gym
  • Has vegetarian food options
  • Free parking space
  • Near to 7-11 store
  • About 30-45 min drive to both Don Mueang and Suvarnabhoomi airport 

Things To Know

  • Our family members stayed here (as part of Kesari group tour) in 2024

Koh-Samui

Property Name - Lub d

City -
Koh-Samui

Type – 4* Hostel/Hotel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • We stayed here in 2020 🙂
  • Neat and clean beach (Chaweng beach)
  • Amazing property with direct beach access, games, two swimming pools and lots of happening activities
  • Vibrant night-life and fire shows on the beach 
  • Board games like TT, Foosball, Air Hockey, Billiards
  • Walking distance to grocery store, restaurants, laundry shops
  • Clean rooms for individuals (as sharing room of hostels), couples, families
  • Separate non smoking rooms and zones

Things To Know

  • Limited breakfast for vegetarians
  • Washing machine is available in common room and can be used on payment basis
  • Only 2 drinking water bottles provided per day. No replacement if you finish

Other nearby options you can consider in Koh-Samui

Ark Bar

Book This Property

Al Resort

Book This Property

Pattaya

Name - Ibis

City - Pattaya

Type – 4* Hotel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • We stayed here in 2020 🙂
  • Centrally located with close access to most of the things
  • Walkable to mall (Terminal-21), beach, marts, museums etc
  • Equipped kitchen
  • 15 minutes drive to railway station / Genova port

Things To Know

  • Lift is from 1st floor (which feels like 2nd floor). You have to carry luggage till there

Phuket

Name - Ramada by Wyndham

City - Phuket

Type – 5* Hotel

Book This Property

What We Liked

  • We stayed here in 2020 🙂
  • Amazing property in close proximity to all happening places
  • Amazing buffet breakfast with lots of options for Indian vegetarians (Poha, Paratha, Puri, Upma, Idli etc)
  • Indian menu available for lunch/dinner and prices are at-par with other restaurants
  • Large rooms, Kids play area
  • 5 – 15 min walk to beach (Patong beach is 700mt and Kalim beach is 1.6km)
  • Bangla road (night life and adult entertainment) is about 10-15 min walk
  • Walkable to street shopping, restaurants, 7-11s, marts, malls etc

Things To Know

  • About 30 min drive from airport and bus terminal

Other nearby options you can consider in Pattaya

Novotel

Book This Property
